title: polystylus cornu-cervi
intro:
Polystylus cornu-cervi, also known as the Deer Horn tree or Rau Chiew, is a type of plant native to Southeast Asia, specifically Thailand and Cambodia, known for its unique horn-shaped twigs and leaves.

This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. It has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as to improve cardiovascular health and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of Polystylus cornu-cervi include teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
